item.title: AbbVie Humira & TrumpRx
item.description:
• Summary: The White House has added AbbVie's Humira (adalimumab) to the TrumpRx program alongside cheaper biosimilars, offering it at a steep discount amid tariff threats. This policy move aims to increase access to the TNF inhibitor while promoting biosimilar competition in autoimmune disease treatment.
  • MoAs: Humira (adalimumab) is a monoclonal antibody that binds to tumor necrosis factor-alpha (TNFα), inhibiting its interaction with p55 and p75 cell surface receptors, thereby reducing inflammation in conditions like rheumatoid arthritis, psoriasis, and inflammatory bowel disease.
  • Sources: Endpoints News, BioSpace

item.title: FDA Budget & Regulatory Proposals
item.description:
• Summary: The FDA has proposed its 2027 budget, seeking legislative authority for permanent rare disease vouchers, easier entry to clinical trials, expanded regulation of post-approval manufacturing changes, and enhanced crackdown on misleading direct-to-consumer drug ads. These proposals aim to streamline drug development and strengthen regulatory oversight in the biopharma industry.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: STAT News, BioSpace, Fierce Pharma, BioSpace

item.title: Insulin Cap Impact Study
item.description:
• Summary: A study suggests that the $35 monthly insulin cap has increased patient access to insulin, highlighting the positive effects of pricing policies on diabetes management and affordability.
  • MoAs: Insulin is a peptide hormone that binds to the insulin receptor, promoting glucose uptake and storage, and inhibiting hepatic glucose production.
  • Sources: Endpoints News

item.title: Health Tech Funding & AI Regulation
item.description:
• Summary: Coverage of Q1 funding trends in health tech and regulatory discussions on AI, including UnitedHealth Group's AI investments and state-level efforts to regulate healthcare chatbots.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: Endpoints News, STAT News

item.title: Pharmalot Roundup (Adam Feuerstein)
item.description:
• Summary: News roundup covers FDA support for domestic production, a Gilead deal, Merck HIV updates, and Anthropic's role, reflecting broader industry dynamics and regulatory developments.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: STAT News
